Basic fixture crate used by the sourcey-rustdoc adapter tests. Exercises the minimum surface Phase 1 needs: a free function, a struct with an inherent impl, a trait with one method, a trait impl on the struct, and two doctests (one with hidden setup lines and an explicit `main` wrapper, one plain).

Macros §

hello
Source

hello §

macro_rules! hello

Greeting macro: expands to a hello string.

Examples

let s = basic::hello!("world");
assert_eq!(s, "hello, world");
ignore
let s = basic::hello!("world");
assert_eq!(s, "hello, world");

Structs §

Widget
Source

Widget §

pub struct basic::Widget { /* ... */ }

A widget that can be published.

Examples

# use basic::Widget;
# fn main() {
let mut w = Widget::new("hero");
w.publish();
assert!(w.is_published());
# }
let mut w = Widget::new("hero");
w.publish();
assert!(w.is_published());

use basic::Widget;
fn main() {
let mut w = Widget::new("hero");
w.publish();
assert!(w.is_published());
}
Run

Implementations §

impl Widget

new

Source
fn new(name: impl Into<String>) -> Self

Construct a new widget with the given name.

Examples

use basic::Widget;

let w = Widget::new("alpha");
assert!(!w.is_published());
use basic::Widget;

let w = Widget::new("alpha");
assert!(!w.is_published());
Run

publish

Source
fn publish(self: &mut Self)

Mark the widget as published.

Examples

use basic::Widget;

let mut w = Widget::new("beta");
w.publish();
assert!(w.is_published());
use basic::Widget;

let mut w = Widget::new("beta");
w.publish();
assert!(w.is_published());
Run

is_published

Source
fn is_published(self: &Self) -> bool

Returns true if publish has been called.

impl Borrow for Widget

borrow

fn borrow(self: &Self) -> &T

impl BorrowMut for Widget

borrow_mut

fn borrow_mut(self: &mut Self) -> &mut T

impl Into for Widget

into

fn into(self: Self) -> U

Calls U::from(self).

That is, this conversion is whatever the implementation of From<T> for U chooses to do.

impl From for Widget

from

fn from(t: T) -> T

Returns the argument unchanged.

impl TryInto for Widget

Error

try_into

fn try_into(self: Self) -> Result<U, <U as TryFrom<T>>::Error>

impl TryFrom for Widget

Error

try_from

fn try_from(value: U) -> Result<T, <T as TryFrom<U>>::Error>

impl Any for Widget

type_id

fn type_id(self: &Self) -> TypeId

impl Introducer for Widget

introduce

Source
fn introduce(self: &Self) -> String
LegacyWidget
Source

LegacyWidget §

pub struct basic::LegacyWidget { /* ... */ }
👎 Deprecated since 0.0.2: use Widget instead

An old wrapper that has been replaced by Widget.

Examples

// Forces a panic at runtime for documentation purposes.
let _w: basic::LegacyWidget = panic!("legacy");
should_panic
// Forces a panic at runtime for documentation purposes.
let _w: basic::LegacyWidget = panic!("legacy");
Run

Implementations §

impl Borrow for LegacyWidget

borrow

fn borrow(self: &Self) -> &T

impl BorrowMut for LegacyWidget

borrow_mut

fn borrow_mut(self: &mut Self) -> &mut T

impl Into for LegacyWidget

into

fn into(self: Self) -> U

Calls U::from(self).

That is, this conversion is whatever the implementation of From<T> for U chooses to do.

impl From for LegacyWidget

from

fn from(t: T) -> T

Returns the argument unchanged.

impl TryInto for LegacyWidget

Error

try_into

fn try_into(self: Self) -> Result<U, <U as TryFrom<T>>::Error>

impl TryFrom for LegacyWidget

Error

try_from

fn try_from(value: U) -> Result<T, <T as TryFrom<U>>::Error>

impl Any for LegacyWidget

type_id

fn type_id(self: &Self) -> TypeId

Status §

pub enum basic::Status { /* ... */ }

Status that a widget can be in.

from

Returns the argument unchanged.

type_id

Traits §

Introducer
Source

Introducer §

pub trait basic::Introducer { /* ... */ }

Anything that can introduce itself in one short sentence.

Examples

use basic::{Introducer, Widget};

let w = Widget::new("gamma");
println!("{}", w.introduce());
no_run
use basic::{Introducer, Widget};

let w = Widget::new("gamma");
println!("{}", w.introduce());
Run

Implementations §

Required Methods §

introduce

Source
fn introduce(self: &Self) -> String

Produce the introduction line.

impl Introducer for Widget

introduce

Source
fn introduce(self: &Self) -> String

Functions §

greet
Source

greet §

fn greet(name: &str) -> String

Returns a greeting for the given name.

Examples

use basic::greet;

assert_eq!(greet("Ada"), "hello, Ada");
use basic::greet;

assert_eq!(greet("Ada"), "hello, Ada");
Run
describe
Source

describe §

fn describe(w: &Widget) -> String
#[must_use]

Inspect a widget's display string.

Examples

let _w = basic::Widget::new("delta");
let _d: i32 = _w; // type mismatch on purpose
compile_fail
let _w = basic::Widget::new("delta");
let _d: i32 = _w; // type mismatch on purpose
Run
